deployedGeneratedSources.name: #utility.yul

documentation.text:
============================================================
  LAYER 2 -  AUDIT TRAIL  (Immutable Audit Log & ML Predictions)
 ============================================================
 After data flows through MySQL and the ML pipeline, the results
 are written back to this contract so that:
   1. Every action has a permanent, tamper-proof audit entry.
   2. ML predictions (alerts, anomalies, demand forecasts) are
      stored on-chain for verifiability.
 Flow:  MySQL  ──►  ML Engine  ──►  This contract  ──►  Dashboard
 The Django backend calls these functions via Web3.py after ML
 processing is complete.
@notice Authorise a backend wallet (the Django server's account) to write entries.
@notice Get all audit entry IDs for a base.
@notice Get all ML prediction IDs for a base.
@notice Get all audit entry IDs for a specific asset (vehicle / part).
@notice Get all ML prediction IDs for a specific asset.

typeDescriptions.typeString: tuple(bytes32,enum AuditTrail.ActionCategory,uint256,string storage ref,string storage ref,bytes32,uint256)

documentation.text: @notice Full details of an audit entry.

typeDescriptions.typeString: tuple(bytes32,string storage ref,string storage ref,enum AuditTrail.AlertSeverity,string storage ref,uint256,string storage ref,uint256)

documentation.text: @notice Full details of an ML prediction.
@notice Verify data integrity- compare stored hash with a provided hash.

params._actionSummary: Readable summary, e.g. "Removed 2 vehicles – deployed to border".
params._affectedAsset: Vehicle number or part code (for indexing).
params._category: Which asset category this action belongs to.
params._dataHash: SHA-256 hash of the full MySQL record for integrity verification.
params._layer1RefId: Corresponding entry ID from the AssetLedger contract.
params._performedBy: Username or wallet string of the actor.

params._affectedAsset: Vehicle number or part code affected.
params._recommendedAction: Suggested action for the base admin.

authoriseBackend(address).notice: Authorise a backend wallet (the Django server's account) to write entries.

getAssetAuditTrail(string).notice: Get all audit entry IDs for a specific asset (vehicle / part).

getAssetPredictions(string).notice: Get all ML prediction IDs for a specific asset.

getAuditEntryDetails(uint256).notice: Full details of an audit entry.

getBaseAuditTrail(string).notice: Get all audit entry IDs for a base.

getBasePredictions(string).notice: Get all ML prediction IDs for a base.

getMLPredictionDetails(uint256).notice: Full details of an ML prediction.

logAuditEntry(string,uint8,uint256,strin.notice: Write an immutable audit entry after MySQL persistence.

storeMLPrediction(string,string,string,u.notice: Store an ML prediction on-chain for verifiability.

verifyDataIntegrity(uint256,bytes32).notice: Verify data integrity- compare stored hash with a provided hash.

userdoc.notice: ============================================================  LAYER 2 -  AUDIT TRAIL  (Immutable Audit Log & ML Predictions) ============================================================ After data flows through MySQL and the ML pipeline, the results are written back to this contract so that:   1. Every action has a permanent, tamper-proof audit entry.   2. ML predictions (alerts, anomalies, demand forecasts) are      stored on-chain for verifiability. Flow:  MySQL  ──►  ML Engine  ──►  This contract  ──►  Dashboard The Django backend calls these functions via Web3.py after ML processing is complete.
